entry.title: Re: SAP Multibank Connectivity Integration: BASIS Perspective
entry.content:
Hi,

 

From a functional perspective, for setting up SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity (MBC), the key information required from the bank typically includes:

 

- Connectivity details (SFTP / EBICS / SWIFT or other agreed protocol)
- Endpoint details (host, port, directories or API endpoints)
- Authentication method (username/password or SSH key exchange)
- Message formats supported (for example, payment formats like ISO 20022 XML)
- File naming conventions and folder structure
- Encryption and signing requirements (if applicable)

 

SAP MBC acts as a communication layer, so alignment with the bank on these technical and format requirements is essential for successful integration.

 

Further reference:
SAP Help Portal – SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity 
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MULTI_BANK_CONNECTIVITY

 

Regards 
Mahendra C
MBC Team

entry.title: SAC Planning – Show ACTUAL and WORKING in Same Table with Editable WORKING Only
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have a requirement in SAP Analytics Cloud Planning.

Current setup:

  • Rows = Account

  • Columns = Version (ACTUAL, WORKING)

Requirement:

  • Both ACTUAL and WORKING versions should be visible in the same table.

  • ACTUAL should be read-only.

  • WORKING should be editable.

Issue:
I tried using an Input Control on the Version dimension and selected only WORKING. However, the Input Control filters the entire table widget, so ACTUAL gets removed from the table.

Is there any standard approach or best practice in SAC to:

  • Display both ACTUAL and WORKING in the same table

  • Keep ACTUAL read-only

  • Allow editing only for WORKING

Any suggestions would be appreciated.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: ewm inbound delivery change item vendor batch and pro...
entry.content:
Good day  @gurumoorthy4 

Thank you for your question.

I would not recommend /SCWM/RFUI and any unreleased BAPI as a correction point in your scenario.

For Public Cloud, first use Change Inbound Deliveries to correct item level batch information before goods receipt.         

If the value belongs to the batch master, use Create Batch/Manage Batches or a released Batch Master Record API where possible.   

 If your warehouse tasks or goods receipt are already processed, reverse the relevant process steps first, correct the delivery or batch master and then reprocess the warehouse task and goods receipt.  

 

See the Blog for more insights: Batch Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition for more insights.

If the issue you are facing is about how batch creation, batch classification, or expiration date checks are controlled during inbound delivery processing, then consider checking configuration activity 102338.

Here are references for more guidance

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/546ac23df1254af3baca5d9afe99ed04.html?locale=en-US

3515363 - Batch creation in Change Inbound Deliveries APP without BBD - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

3718255 - How to control batch creation in inbound delivery - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: #CoE #DH01 Muti-Bank Connectivity with S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

 

If the requirement is to retrieve only bank account balance (without full transaction statements), this is typically not handled as a separate flow in SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity.

 

In MBC scenarios, bank account balances are usually provided as part of inbound bank statement messages (for example, CAMT.052 intraday or CAMT.053 end-of-day statements).

 

These messages are then processed in SAP S/4HANA and used for cash management and reporting.

 

There is no standalone MBC process to fetch only balance data independently of bank statement messages. The balance information is derived from the statement files provided by the bank.

 

Further information:
SAP Help Portal – SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity 
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MULTI_BANK_CONNECTIVITY

 

Regards 
Mahendra C
MBC Team

entry.title: Re: multi bank receipts entries upload
entry.content:
Hi,

 

Uploading multiple bank receipt entries via Excel/XML is typically handled on the SAP ERP side, not directly by SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity.

 

In MBC scenarios:
- MBC is used to transmit files between banks and SAP systems
- It does not provide functionality to upload or post receipt entries directly

 

For your requirement, common approaches are:
- Use Electronic Bank Statement (EBS) processing (for example via FF.5) using bank files like MT940 / CAMT formats
- Use standard upload tools (such as LSMW, BAPI, or custom programs) to post receipt entries from Excel/XML

 

If the data is coming from a bank, it is recommended to use standard bank statement formats, which can be sent via MBC and then processed in SAP.

 

Further reference:
SAP Help Portal – SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity 
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MULTI_BANK_CONNECTIVITY

 

Regards 
Mahendra C
MBC Team

entry.title: Re: Multi Bank connectivity
entry.content:
Hi,

 

In SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity (MBC) scenarios, the configuration for host‑to‑host connectivity is split between ERP system setup and MBC onboarding.

 

From the ERP side, the key configuration typically includes:
- Setting up SSL certificates (STRUST) for secure communication
- Maintaining HTTP-based RFC destinations (SM59) for connectivity
- Configuring routing settings and pull types in the MBC connector
- Maintaining Sender ID / Receiver ID for message routing
- Defining inbound and outbound processing (payment files, bank statements)

 

From the MBC side:
- Bank connectivity (SFTP/EBICS/SWIFT) is handled by SAP MBC
- Endpoints, certificates, and technical parameters are provided during onboarding

 

Since MBC is a managed cloud service, much of the connectivity setup with the bank is handled by SAP during onboarding.

 

Further reference:
SAP Help Portal – SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity 
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MULTI_BANK_CONNECTIVITY

 

Regards 
Mahendra C
MBC Team

entry.title: Re: Multi Bank Connectivity Overview
entry.content:
Hi,

 

SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity (MBC) primarily integrates with SAP ERP systems (such as SAP S/4HANA or ECC) through the MBC connector.

 

However, it is also possible to involve non‑SAP systems in the landscape:
- Payment files generated from non‑SAP systems can be sent to SAP via supported integration (for example using file import, APIs, or middleware) and then transmitted to banks via MBC 
- Similarly, bank messages received via MBC can be consumed by non‑SAP systems through appropriate integration layers (for example middleware or custom interfaces)

 

MBC itself acts as a secure communication layer and does not directly integrate with non‑SAP business applications. Integration with non‑SAP systems is typically achieved via SAP ERP or middleware components.

 

Further reference:
SAP Help Portal – SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity 
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MULTI_BANK_CONNECTIVITY

 

Regards 
Mahendra C
MBC Team

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Essentials MBC Bank Payments Automation
entry.content:
Hi,

 

SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity (MBC) does not natively provide automated exchange rate updates.

 

Exchange rate integration is typically handled via other services (such as market data providers or SAP APIs), while MBC is mainly used for payment and bank communication flows.

 

Regards 
Mahendra C
MBC Team

entry.title: Re: SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity Functional Playbook
entry.content:
Hi,

 

The availability of message types (such as MT940) in /BSNAGT/UPLOAD_FILE depends on the configuration maintained in the MBC connector.

 

MT940 will only appear if:
- The message type is defined and active in the system
- Inbound processing steps are maintained for that message type
- A corresponding processing variant (for example, FF.5 for bank statements) is assigned

 

If MT940 is not visible, please check:
SPRO → Multi-Bank Connectivity Connector → Maintain Inbound Processing Steps

 

Also verify that the message type is correctly configured and linked to the appropriate processing logic for bank statement import.

 

For more details:
SAP Help Portal – SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity 
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MULTI_BANK_CONNECTIVITY

 

Regards 
Mahendra C
MBC Team

entry.title: SAP DRC B2B e-Invoicing in Germany – Everything You Need to Know
entry.content:
Germany is moving toward mandatory B2B e-invoicing, driven by both national tax modernization and the broader EU initiative on digital reporting (the VAT in the Digital Age (ViDA) reform package). The key shift is not a single “big bang” mandate in 2028, but a phased transition that already started in 2025, with full structured e-invoicing adoption for domestic B2B transactions expected by the end of the decade.  
As of 1 January 2025, Germany introduced the first binding step: businesses must be able to receive structured electronic invoices in compliance with the legal requirements. This means paper PDFs sent by email are no longer sufficient as the default “invoice standard” between businesses, even though transitional handling is allowed. 

Starting 1 January 2028, Germany enforces mandatory use of electronic invoices for all B2B transactions within the country. This phased approach gives organizations time to align processes, but delays risk operational disruption, penalties, and exclusion from supply chains. 

>>> Want to see it in action? Join SAPs localization experts and get a refresher on how to transition smoothly using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance - registration details at the end <<<

Why ZUGFeRD and XRechnung matter- the two pillars of compliance

Germany effectively converges around two core formats (both compliant with the European semantic standard EN 16931, but differ in implementation style and ecosystem fit): 

  • XRechnung
    This is the fully structured XML standard, originally designed for invoicing public authorities. It is strict, machine-readable, and aligned with EU semantic requirements.
  • ZUGFeRD
    This is a hybrid format: a human-readable PDF/A invoice with an embedded XML data layer. It’s widely used in the private sector because it is easier to adopt incrementally. 

While XRechnung dominates B2G, ZUGFeRD’s hybrid design makes it the preferred choice for B2B due to its dual readability—critical for partners lacking advanced e-invoicing infrastructure.

Practical implication for businesses

If you operate in Germany or invoice German companies, the key takeaway is:

  • PDFs alone will become increasingly non-compliant for B2B transactions
  • You will likely need to support both ZUGFeRD and XRechnung, depending on trading partners
  • ERP and accounting systems must be upgraded well before full enforcement to avoid invoice rejection and payment delays

Call to Action: Germany’s 2025 transitional phase is a strategic window to future-proof your invoicing workflows and choose the suitable tool for your business. SAP Document and Reporting Compliance solutions can help businesses achieve compliance with both XRechnung and Zugferd formats, regardless of the complexity of their invoicing processes. By automating compliance checks, providing customizable templates, integrating with SAP systems, offering reporting and analytics, and providing multilingual support, SAP Document and Reporting Compliance solutions can help businesses streamline their invoicing processes and avoid costly errors.

entry.title: Multiple Cash Clearing Accounts with Single Account Symbol
entry.content:
Multiple Cash Clearing Accounts with Single Account Symbol in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 

EBS Configuration for Japanese Consolidated Billing (締め請求)

Prerequisites

Hello experts,

This question is based on a Japan-specific business requirement: automatic clearing of open receivables during Electronic Bank Statement (EBS) processing for consolidated billing (締め請求). In this process, multiple invoices are aggregated monthly and settled as a single lump-sum payment. The requirement is to accurately auto-clear the corresponding open items during EBS processing.

Background

We are implementing Electronic Bank Statement processing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and encountering challenges with account symbol configuration in the Electronic Bank Statement Global Settings for multiple cash accounts.

Current Configuration

Item Detail

House Bank AccountJPDP1/1EG2 → assigned to G/L Account 11001011 (Ordinary Deposit 1)
Intended MappingCash clearing account linked to house bank G/L account 11001011: 11002080
ErrorThe account symbol +++++++080 in the Electronic Bank Statement Global Settings attempts to post to G/L account 11001080, but house bank account JPDP1/1EG2 is not assigned to G/L account 11001080, resulting in a posting error. We suspect that dynamic derivation based on the house bank account's G/L assignment is not functioning.

Expected Behavior

We expected that the account symbol +++++++080 in the Electronic Bank Statement Global Settings would dynamically resolve to the appropriate clearing account based on the G/L account assigned to the house bank account master data.

Questions

Q1: Dynamic Account Derivation Does SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition support dynamic derivation of the clearing account based on the house bank account's G/L assignment when using a single account symbol (+++++++080) in the Electronic Bank Statement Global Settings?

Q2: Configuration Options in Electronic Bank Statement Global Settings Are there any configuration options within the Electronic Bank Statement Global Settings to support multiple cash clearing accounts — where each house bank account maps to a different clearing account?

Q3: Posting Rule Configuration and Automatic Clearing If we use Posting Rules to route postings to multiple cash clearing accounts, what is the correct configuration approach?

Additionally, we are experiencing the following issue:

  • Without Posting Rules configured: EBS processing correctly auto-clears open receivables during bank statement import
  • After configuring Posting Rules: Auto-clearing of open receivables during EBS processing no longer works

How should Posting Rules be configured to support multiple cash clearing account assignment while preserving automatic clearing for Japanese consolidated billing (締め請求) lump-sum payments?

Technical Details

Item Detail

SystemSAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
ProcessElectronic Bank Statement Processing (Consolidated Billing / 締め請求)
ModuleCash Management
Configuration AppsSSCUI 101024 (Electronic Bank Statement Global Settings), SSCUI 103557 (Assign Account Symbols to Payment Methods)

Current Issue Summary

The account symbol +++++++080 in the Electronic Bank Statement Global Settings is attempting to post to G/L account 11001080 rather than dynamically resolving to the correct clearing account (11002080) based on the house bank account's G/L assignment (11001011), resulting in a posting error.

Furthermore, after configuring Posting Rules, automatic clearing of open receivables is no longer triggered during EBS processing, breaking the auto-clearing requirement for consolidated billing lump-sum payments.

We need to achieve both:

  1. Correct clearing account assignment per house bank account
  2. Automatic clearing of open receivables for consolidated billing payments

Any guidance on the correct configuration approach would be greatly appreciated.

Best regards,

Tamaki

entry.title: Service Bindings with OAuth 2.0 for SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition
entry.content:
This post outlines the procedure to specify client credentials required for SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition systems to communicate with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition, using OAuth 2.0 client authentication. OAuth 2.0 provides a secure, token-based approach that is required for newer integration scenarios with SAP BTP services.

Prerequisites

Before starting the procedure, ensure the following requirements are met:

  • Initial setup in SAP BTP has been performed manually or via the Set Up an Account for SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition booster
  • Required country/region-specific processes are active for your subaccount.
  • SAP Note 3540443 – Electronic Communication Integration Framework: Implementation Overview has been implemented.
  • You have the Space Developer or Org Manager role in your SAP BTP subaccount.

Note: If initial setup for a process or business system is already complete, it does not need to be repeated for additional processes in the same subaccount region.

Step 1: Create the Service Binding

    • Navigate to the Subaccount — In the SAP BTP cockpit, go to the subaccount where the service binding will be created.

 

    • Access Instances and Subscriptions: Choose Services > Instances and Subscriptions.

 

    • Select Service Instance: Select the service instance created during the initial setup of the cloud edition.

 

    • Create Service Binding: In the Service Bindings section on the right, choose Create and fill in the fields as follows:

 

  • Finalize: Choose Create.

Special Instructions for France (E-Invoices and E-Reports)

If integrating to activate the E-Invoices and E-Reports (France) process, repeat the steps above but select 'Peppol Exchange' as the process instead of 'Other Processes' in the binding configuration.

 

Step 2: Retrieve the OAuth 2.0 Credentials

After creating the service binding, you need to retrieve the credentials to configure the OAuth 2.0 client in your SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition system.

    • Access Instances and Subscriptions — In the SAP BTP cockpit, in the navigation area, choose Services > Instances and Subscriptions.

 

    • Select Service Instance — Under Instances, choose the service instance of the cloud edition. The details screen for the service instance expands on the right side.

 

    • View Credentials — In the details screen, choose a service binding name to open the Credentials screen.

 

  • Retrieve Details — The Credentials screen displays the url, clientId, clientSecret, and drccApiUrl for this service binding.

Note: You need these credentials in the next steps when configuring the OAuth 2.0 client.

Summary
You have now successfully created a service binding using OAuth 2.0 client authentication in SAP BTP and retrieved the credentials needed for the integration. The next step is to use the clientId, clientSecret, url, and drccApiUrl to configure the OAuth 2.0 client in your SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition system, which will enable secure communication with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition.

The mapping: Movement Type → Value String → Transaction Key

entry.title: Re: Issue Maintaining 19-Digit Bank Account Number for Cu...
entry.content:
Good day @rohini_dutta21 

Thank you for your question.

Question 1: Is it possible in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to maintain a bank account number with more than 18 digits in the Business Partner app?

Answer: Yes, but not fully in the Bank Account Number field alone. The standard approach is for you to split the long bank account number:

  • Make sure you enter the first 18 digits in the Bank Account Number field.
  • Then enter the remaining digit/s in the reference Details field.

For your example of a 19-digit account number, Bank Account Number = first 18 digits and Reference Details = 19th digit. See KBA 2890216 for details on long bank account number in Business Partner bank details.

 

Question 2: Is there any configuration, SSCUI, or workaround available to achieve this requirement?

Answer:  There is no SSCUI that changes the technical length of the standard Bank Account Number field beyond 18 characters. The country specific setting you changed are used for country specific bank checks, such as bank account number length validation, bank number length, and some other formal checks. See KBA 3700281 for more details.

However, this type of setting does not increase the technical field length of BUT0BK-BANKN. This is why changing the setting from 18 to 19 does not allow the full 19 digit value to be entered in the Bank Account Number field.

So, the supported workaround is as follows:

  1. Maintain the first 18 digits in Bank Account Number
  2. Maintain the remaining digit in Reference Details.
  3. If you require payment file, confirm that the relevant payment medium or bank file format can concatenate both fields correctly.

KBA 3320618 also describes a follow up issue where long bank account numbers are maintained with the remaining digits in Reference Details.

 

Question 3: Are there any SAP notes or standard limitations related to this behavior?

Answer: Yes. The limitation as mentioned above is that the standard bank account number field has a maximum length of 18 characters. See Note 1585003 and KBA 3362941 for more insights,

I recommend the following:

  1. Open the designated customer in Manage Business Partner Master Data. Go to Payment Transactions.                               
  2. Maintain the first 18 digits in Bank Account Number and maintain the remaining digit in Reference Details.           
  3. Save the BP
  4. Test the relevant payment file, report, or downstream app to confirm whether both fields are considered.
  5. If the output does not show or use Reference Details, check the relevant payment format or you my consider raising a case with the payment format and country details.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Nachträgliches Ergänzen der Partnergesellschaft in bereits gebuchten Forderungen
entry.content:
Hallo,

folgender Anwendungsfall im Bereich Debitoren / IC-Abstimmung:

Die Debitoren werden über eine Schnittstelle in SAP angelegt. Die Partnergesellschaft wird dabei jedoch nicht automatisch mitgegeben, sondern bei Bedarf manuell im Debitorenstamm gepflegt. Hintergrund ist, dass die Partnergesellschaft grundsätzlich bereits für alle relevanten Debitoren vorhanden sein sollte.

Es kommt jedoch vor, dass neue Kunden über die Schnittstelle angelegt werden und dabei die Partnergesellschaft fehlt. Dies fällt teilweise erst später auf, wenn bereits Forderungen auf diesen Debitor gebucht wurden.

Das Problem ist dann, dass die bereits gebuchten Forderungen keine Partnergesellschaft enthalten und dadurch nicht in der IC-Abstimmung erscheinen.

Meine Frage ist daher:

Gibt es eine Möglichkeit, die Partnergesellschaft nachträglich in bereits gebuchten offenen Posten bzw. Forderungen zu ergänzen, wenn sie erst nachträglich im Debitorenstamm gepflegt wurde?

Falls ja: Gibt es hierfür bspw. einen Job, der die Partnergesellschaft aus dem Debitorenstamm nachträglich in bestehende Belege / offene Posten übernimmt?

entry.title: Shape the Future of Purchase Orders in SAP S/4HANA Cloud — Join Our Customer Engagement Initiative
entry.content:
Are you working with Purchase Orders in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition? We want to hear from you.

SAP is launching a Customer Engagement Initiative (CEI) focused on the evolution of the Purchase Order experience — and we are inviting customers and partners to be part of the journey from day one.

What is a CEI?

A Customer Engagement Initiative is SAP's way of bringing customers and partners directly into the development process — not after decisions are made, but while they are still being shaped. As a participant, you get a seat at the table: early access to concept ideas, prototypes and solution approaches, with the opportunity to give structured feedback that directly influences the SAP product roadmap.

What's This CEI About?

Purchase Orders are at the heart of procurement — and we know that the way people work with them is evolving. This initiative will look at how customers actually use the Purchase Order applications today: the processes, the challenges and the opportunities for improvement.

A particular focus will be on AI-driven enhancements and intelligent automation — we will be sharing early-stage ideas and planned innovations in this space and want your honest input on whether they address real operational needs and deliver genuine business value.

This is your chance to make sure the next generation of SAP procurement capabilities reflects how you actually work.

Why Should You Join?

  • Influence the roadmap early — your feedback shapes what gets built, not just how it is polished
  • Get a preview of what is coming — including AI use cases and modernization concepts before they go public
  • Connect directly with SAP development teams — a direct line to SAP Product Management and Development
  • Be heard — the insights gathered through this CEI will serve as direct input for SAP's product direction in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition

How to Register

Registration is open now and closes on June 19, 2026.

Register here: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#campaign/4229

If you are a customer or partner working with procurement in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, we would love to have you involved.

entry.title: Re: Interest calcuation on Customer Open Items with 30/60...
entry.content:
Why this happens is because, Tolerance Days in SAP are designed to suppress interest calculation if the key date falls within the tolerance window. After tolerance days are over, the system resumes interest calculation from the original due date, not shifted forward. This is standard system behavior in Public Cloud and differs from what you want (interest starting only after Due Date + 30/60/90 days).

Options to meet your requirement

1) Custom Interest Calculation Logic (CDS + RAP Extension)
Build a custom CDS view to calculate “Adjusted Due Date = Net Due Date + Grace Days.” Use this adjusted date as the basis for interest calculation in your custom report or app. This avoids relying on tolerance days and gives you full control.

2) Alternative Configuration
Instead of tolerance days, configure different interest calculation variants for 30/60/90 days. Assign these variants based on customer/vendor group or document type. This way, the system calculates interest only after the adjusted due date.

3) Business Rule Framework (BRF+)
In Public Cloud, BRF+ can be used to define rules for interest calculation. You can set conditions: “If overdue days > 30, start interest from Due Date + 30,” etc. This gives flexibility without modifying core interest calculation logic.

entry.title: Re: ISU Billing a meter with 3 registers
entry.content:
@ricky_shaw  

Yes — the time slices are defined inside the rate, and they work together with the TOU key you maintain in the installation facts.

Here are the Step to define the time slices : You define them in the Rate Steps under your rate in:

EA30 → Rate → Time-of-Use / Time Slices

This is where you configure:

ON‑PEAK window (e.g., 14:00–20:00)

OFF‑PEAK window (e.g., 20:00–14:00)

Any seasonal or weekend variations

Priority of slices if they overlap

These time slices tell the billing engine which register applies at which timestamp.

in case if you need TOU?
The TOU key links the meter’s registers to the time slices you defined in the rate.
Once the TOU key is stored in ES32 → Installation Facts, billing automatically knows:

which register = ON‑PEAK

which register = OFF‑PEAK

which rate step to apply based on the timestamp

This is the standard IS‑U design for TOU meters with multiple registers.

entry.title: Re: Combined Credit/Debit Note Posting Against Multiple I...
entry.content:
Please find below answers to your questions

1. Is this requirement supported in standard SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition / GROW with SAP?
No. Standard functionality supports invoice-level credit/debit memos for rate difference adjustments. Consolidation across multiple invoices into a single memo is not delivered.

2. Can one credit memo or debit memo be created with reference to multiple invoices in this scenario?
Not directly in standard. Each memo references a single invoice for compliance and document flow reasons. Multiple invoice references are not supported in the delivered process.

3. Is it possible to net both credit and debit adjustments and create only one final credit/debit memo document?
Not in standard. Netting across invoices is not supported because tax and audit rules require invoice-level adjustments. The system calculates and posts separately.

4. If not available in standard, is there a recommended SAP-approved approach?
Yes. SAP recommends extensibility approaches:

  • Custom Logic (BAdI in Public Cloud): Intercept billing and aggregate adjustments for selected customers.
  • Side-by-Side Extension (SAP BTP): Build a consolidation app that nets adjustments and triggers a single billing document via API.
  • Custom Billing Type: Define a special billing type for consolidated memos.
  • Key User Extensibility: Add custom fields to store references to multiple invoices.

5. Are there any limitations related to billing type, document flow, tax calculation, accounting posting, invoice reference, or audit compliance?
Yes:

  • Billing Type: Needs to be defined for consolidated memos.
  • Document Flow: Standard flow supports one-to-one linkage; custom linkage must be built.
  • Tax Calculation: Must ensure correct recalculation on netted amounts.
  • Accounting Posting: Consolidated postings must still balance correctly.
  • Audit Compliance: Auditors may require invoice-level detail; consolidated memos must retain references to original invoices.

So the conclusion is

Standard SAP GROW does not support this requirement.

To achieve it, you need custom extensibility:

  • Use Custom Logic (BAdI) or SAP BTP side-by-side extension.
  • Create a special billing type for consolidated memos.
  • Maintain invoice references via custom fields or attachments.

This ensures compliance while meeting the business need for selected customers.

entry.title: ABAP FS: Three Months Later
entry.content:
“The greatest trick ABAP FS ever pulled was convincing developers that Copilot was doing all the magic.”

A bit of background

Marcello Urbani’s ABAP Remote Filesystem extension has enabled ABAP development in VS Code since 2018. I still have no idea how he did that. He brought ABAP development to VS Code long before SAP seriously considered it.

A year ago, I started building what is now ABAP FS v2. It was supposed to be a casual side project/experiment that ended up consuming most of my time and thoughts. AI enables you to build things quickly - which is good but also bad because there’s always something more to build and it never ends!

V2 goes open-source

Three months ago, I open-sourced v2 so it could improve and benefit the entire ABAP development community (and to put my name behind something meaningful, of course).

We’re proud to share that ABAP FS now has 290+ GitHub stars and 53K activeinstalls - gaining 10K new active installs since v2 went live! And this doesn’t even include offline versions probably floating around inside enterprise environments!

What has happened since

The last three months have been very exciting.

ABAP FS has gained 15+ new features across 26 releases, including REPL, Notebooks, Heartbeat, Replay debugger, Skills, subagents and MCP support.

The community has reported 40+ issues, of which 15 were real bugs that have since been fixed.

We’ve also had 4 new contributors - Gurjant Singh, Özgür, Oláh Tamás, and Sébastien along with 4 new positive reviews in the VS Code extension marketplace.

The rough edges

AI enables you to move faster and experiment easily - not every feature is perfect and that’s part of the process. Features like the Notebooks and replay debugger are as much about proving what’s possible for ABAP as they’re about solving problems today. Once you know something is possible, even if it’s not perfect, it opens the door for the community and maybe even SAP to build something better!

What’s next

ABAP FS currently only supports Basic authentication which is a problem. So we’re adding support for other authentication methods like SSO, Kerberos, etc - coming soon!

I also feel the need to address both the elephants in the room:

SAP’s new API policy - Everyone’s talking about it and there are several interpretations of it depending on who you ask. If you ask me, the policy restricts accessing real business data using ADT APIs but you can use them for their intended purpose, which is ABAP development. Until SAP makes the position clearer, I think it’s safer to proceed carefully and stick to development scenarios.

SAP’s official VS Code extension - Finally. From what I hear, it’s gonna be free to use and will eventually/hopefully have full parity with Eclipse ADT. I hope it’s good and useful. Yes, I will probably be sad if and when ABAP FS becomes unnecessary but I hope it has a meaningful purpose while it’s alive - make ABAP development faster, more intelligent, and less frustrating.

Thank you

First - Thank you Marcello. None of this would be possible without you. Thank you for sharing your work and your expertise with the world! Collaborating with you has been a pleasure and a privilege!

A huge thanks to Gurjant Singh, Özgür, Oláh Tamás, and Sébastien for your contributions.

Thanks to everyone who’ve tried ABAP FS, reviewed it, reported issues, provided feedback, blogged, and told friends and colleagues about it.

We would love to hear how you’ve been using ABAP FS, what’s working well and especially what’s not!

entry.title: Re: Schedule automatic payment - Payment medium - Payment...
entry.content: Standard NOVA process is Payment file is generated at payment run. By reassigning activities, you can generate the payment file at proposal stage (for validation), and suppress file creation at payment run (only posting + advice). This is achievable through authorization/activity profile adjustments as you have outlined. If suppression of XML file creation at payment run is not fully supported in Public Cloud, then a custom logic extension may be required.

entry.title: Live Session: Get Certified with Confidence: SAP Customer Data Cloud Implementation Consultant
entry.content:
Are you ready to take the next big step in your SAP career? Join our Get Certified live session series designed specifically to help you achieve the SAP Certified – Implementation Consultant – SAP Customer Data Cloud (C_C4H62) certification.

This comprehensive, expert-led series is your guided pathway to mastering key concepts, strengthening your practical knowledge, and building the confidence you need to pass the certification exam successfully.

Why Join This Live Session Series?

Preparing for certification can feel overwhelming—but you don’t have to do it alone. In this interactive series, experienced SAP trainers will walk you through critical topics, provide real-world insights, and answer your questions live.

Whether you're a Consultant, Architect, or Administrator, this series is designed to help you bridge the gap between theory and practical application.

What’s Included in the Series?

 Kick-off Session (1 hour)

Start strong with a structured introduction to:

  • Certification scope and format
  • Exam interface and expectations
  • Proven preparation strategies
  • Learning journey overview and study materials
  • Practice system guidance

Content Review Sessions

Dive deep into the most important certification topics through 4 focused sessions, expertly designed to complement your self-study:

Transforming Customer Engagement: CIAM for B2C (2 hours)

Learn how to leverage SAP Customer Data Cloud for modern, secure customer identity and access management in B2C scenarios.

Mastering Data Privacy and Compliance (1 hr 30 mins)

Explore SAP Customer Data Cloud ECPM and understand how to manage consent, preferences, and compliance effectively.

Seamless Customer Identity Management with REST APIs (2 hours)

Gain hands-on insights into extending and integrating SAP Customer Data Cloud using REST APIs.

Mastering SAP Customer Data Cloud CIAM for B2B: The Ultimate Guide  (1 hr 30 mins)

Understand advanced identity management strategies tailored for business-to-business use cases.

Learning Objectives

By the end of this series, you will:

  • Clearly understand the format and scope of the SAP Certified - Implementation Consultant - SAP Customer Data Cloud certification 
  • Be equipped with practical knowledge and expert guidance
  • Feel confident and fully prepared to attempt the certification exam

How to Prepare

To maximize your success:

  • Complete the full Learning Journey associated with the certification
  • Actively participate in all live sessions
  • Practice with hands-on exercises in the SAP Practice System

 

📅Live Session Kick-off

Get Certified: SAP Certified – Implementation Consultant – SAP Customer Data Cloud
🗓September 16, 2026
⏰07:00 – 08:00 (UTC)

Take the Next Step in Your Career

SAP Customer Data Cloud expertise is in high demand as organizations prioritize secure, personalized customer experiences. Becoming certified demonstrates your ability to implement, configure, and optimize this powerful solution.

👉Don’t miss this opportunity to learn from experts, clarify your doubts, and fast-track your certification journey.

entry.title: New BAdI in S/4HANA DRC Post Tax Payable for Joint Venture Cost Object Flexibility
entry.content:
The program RFUMSV00 supports Joint Venture Accounting (JVA) functionality. When JVA is active for a company code, the program displays the Joint Venture name and Equity Group in its output for the corresponding accounting document.

RFUMSV00 also provides a checkbox called "Post to Original Cost Object" on its selection screen. The cost object determination logic depends on the combination of this checkbox and the Recovery Indicator value:

Post to original cost objects     Recovery Indicator                 Result
                      X                 Blank    Original cost objects
                     X            Value filled    Original cost objects
                 Blank                Blank    Original cost objects
                 Blank           Value filled   Alternative cost objects

In short, the system fetches alternative cost objects only when the checkbox is left blank and a Recovery Indicator value is provided. In all other scenarios, original cost objects are used.

The Gap in Post Tax Payable (TPP)

While JVA support was extended to TPP through SAP Note 3643896 — enabling the Joint Venture name and Equity Group to appear in TPP output — a second gap remained. The "Post to Original Cost Object" checkbox, which is readily available on the RFUMSV00 selection screen, was not supported in TPP. Since TPP does not own the OData service, it was not feasible to expose this checkbox on the TPP user interface.

The Solution: BAdI SRF_TPP_JVA_ORIGL_COST_OBJ

To address the above mentioned gap, a new Business Add-In (BAdI) has been introduced: SRF_TPP_JVA_ORIGL_COST_OBJ.

This BAdI allows customers to supply a Recovery Indicator value through their own implementation. Since the checkbox cannot be surfaced on the TPP interface, the field "Post to Original Cost Object" defaults to blank within the BAdI framework. The system also checks whether JVA is active for a particular company code (and the document splitting should be active for that company code) using the TPP class CL_SRF_ACTVT_TAX_PAYABLE.

When a Recovery Indicator is supplied via the BAdI, the system follows the same determination logic as RFUMSV00 and fetches the alternative cost objects tied to that indicator. If no Recovery Indicator is provided, original cost objects continue to be used — ensuring TPP behaves consistently with RFUMSV00.

BAdI Technical Details

  • BAdI Definition: SRF_TPP_JVA_ORIGL_COST_OBJ
  • Enhancement Spot: ES_SRF_TPP_JVA_ORIGL_COST_OBJ
  • BAdI Interface: IF_SRF_TPP_JVA_ORIGL_COST_OBJ
  • Method: SPLIT_TAX_LINES
  • Example Class: CL_SRF_TPP_JVA_COST_OBJ_EX

Filter Values:

  • Reporting Entity — scope the implementation by entity
  • Report Category — scope the implementation by report category

Parameters

Input parameters provided to the BAdI:

  • DOCUMENT_HEADER — fetched from BKPF using the company code, accounting document, and fiscal year within the method GET_SPLITTED_INFO.
  • TAXITEM_DATA — BSET data, retrieved based on the BKPF data in the same method.

Output parameter returned from the BAdI:

  • SPLIT_TAX — the resulting split tax data from the BAdI implementation.

Default Values

The following parameters are pre-filled with default values unless explicitly changed by the customer:

  • POSTING_DATE = current system date (sy-datum)
  • POST_TO_ORIG_COST_OBJECT = ' ' (blank)
  • VENTURE_RANGE = empty
  • EQUITYGROUP_RANGE = empty

Additionally, the method cl_jva_enrich_tax_lines=>get_instance()→split carries these defaults:

  • IV_USE_COUNTRY_CURR = ' ' (blank)
  • IV_BATCH_INPUT = 'X'

How to Implement This BAdI

  1. Create your own implementation of the BAdI SRF_TPP_JVA_ORIGL_COST_OBJ.
  2. Refer to the example class CL_SRF_TPP_JVA_COST_OBJ_EX — this provides a ready-made reference for your implementation.
  3. Copy the code from the example class method into your implementation. 

            4. Provide the value for the Recovery Indicator parameter (IV_SUBST_COST_OBJ_RI), which is passed to the method cl_jva_enrich_tax_lines=>get_instance()→split.

Note: How you determine the Recovery Indicator value is entirely flexible. You can use a configuration parameter, a hard-coded value, pull it from standard customizing, or derive it from any other source that fits your business needs.

entry.title: Re: Need logic for Custom report in SAP PM/EAM Equipment...
entry.content:
#GenAI-Generated Content

Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. Can you please check whether the following procedure works for you?

To calculate the ‘Actual Progress’ field in SAP PM/EAM for a custom report, you can follow the logic outlined below:

Logic for ‘Actual Progress’

  1. Fetch Running Hours:

    • Use the attribute name (ATNAM) ‘RUNNING_HOURS’ to get the attribute internal number (ATINN) from the CABN table.
    • Use the equipment number (EQUNR) and ATINN to find the corresponding measuring point (POINT) in the IMPTT table.
    • Use this POINT to fetch records from the IMRG table within the selected date range (IDATE).
    • Filter these records by each day and consider the CDIFF (difference value) for the measuring point.
    • Group the records by IDATE (Activity Date) and calculate the SUM of CDIFF for each date.

  2. Calculate Actual Progress:

    • The ‘Actual Progress’ can be determined by comparing the cumulative running hours (SUM of CDIFF) against the expected activity progress.
    • Use the formula: Actual Progress = (Cumulative Running Hours / Total Activity Required) * 100 to get the progress percentage.

This logic will help you track the actual progress of activities based on the equipment’s running hours and the total activity required.

Kind regards,

Henrike
